top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 93, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic HalvVättern's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
54ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
HalvVättern's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 93 miles.
5,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of HalvVättern. Train it until it feels boring.
At HalvVättern, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 93 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 93 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,000ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 93 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.heat: June in Motala, Sweden can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before HalvVättern. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for HalvVättern’s 93 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 93 miles of mixed terrain in Motala, Sweden.
